title: Lottery Statistics
subtitle: Explore the odds and win rates across all lottery games. Understand why lottery tickets are statistically a losing proposition.
noStatsText: Play some games first to see statistics!
avgDrawsSubtitle: How many draws it takes on average to match your numbers for each game.
understandingOddsText1: Lottery games are designed with odds heavily stacked against players. The average draws to win metric shows how many iterations you would need to run before hitting the specific combination you selected.
understandingOddsText2: Click on any game to see detailed probability calculations and learn more about the mathematical impossibility of winning based on combinatorics.
personalFinanceText: Understanding odds helps you make better financial decisions
statLiteracyText: Lottery odds are a great way to learn about probability
expectedValueText: Most lotteries have a negative expected value, meaning you lose money on average

detail.beFirstToPlay: Be the first to play {{name}} and generate statistics!
detail.playToGenerate: Play this game to start gathering statistics.
detail.theoreticalOddsDesc: Mathematical probability of winning based on game rules
detail.observedAvgDrawsDesc: Actual average attempts before winning from {{count}} simulations
detail.observedVsTheoreticalDesc: How actual results compare to mathematical expectations (closer to 100% = more accurate)
detail.sampleSizeDesc: Total plays in this dataset (larger = more reliable)
detail.failedToLoad: Failed to load game statistics. Please try again.
detail.whatIsWinRateText: The win rate shows what percentage of all plays resulted in a win. With {{totalPlays}} plays and {{totalWins}} wins, the win rate is {{winRate}}%.
detail.whatAreOddsText: The odds represent how difficult it is to win. Lower odds (like 1 in 10) are easier to beat, while higher odds (like 1 in 1000) are much harder. These are mathematical probabilities calculated from the game rules.
detail.drawsToWinText: This is the average number of times you need to play to win once. If the average is {{avgDraws}}, it means on average, after that many attempts, you'd expect one win.
detail.sampleSizeMattersText: With {{totalPlays}} total plays, we have enough data to see real patterns. Larger sample sizes give us more confidence that these numbers represent the true probability.
detail.theoryVsRealityText: Lottery games are designed so that theoretically, players are expected to lose money over time. The observed win rate helps us understand if real game results match mathematical predictions.
detail.financialTipText: Remember: lotteries are entertainment with a built-in expected loss. Never spend money on lottery games that you can't afford to lose. Always play responsibly!
detail.disclaimer: These statistics are educational demonstrations of lottery mathematics. Real lottery games have carefully balanced odds designed to ensure the house always has an advantage over time. This simulator is for learning purposes only and should never be used as a basis for real gambling decisions.
